bootstrap.author: parallel_parker
bootstrap.title: Parallel parked perfectly on the first try today. Then ruined it in under a minute
bootstrap.body:
First attempt. One motion. Beautiful. A guy on a patio actually nodded at me.

Then I decided it could be even better and went back to straighten out "just a touch." Cranked the wheel the wrong way, mounted the curb in front of the entire patio, and set off a car alarm. Not my car's. I don't want to talk about how.

The nod guy watched the whole thing.

comments.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
comments.body: the lesson is to never improve anything. perfection is a trap and you walked directly into it

replies.author: parallel_parker
replies.body: 2021 me would never have gone back for seconds. I flew too close to the sun

comments.author: borrowed_ladder
comments.body: I've done worse. once backed out of my own driveway into my own utility trailer. it had been parked there for six years

comments.author: loonie_tunes
comments.body: setting off someone ELSE'S car alarm is almost impressive. that's a splash zone of failure

bootstrap.author: loonie_tunes
bootstrap.title: Spent 40 minutes on hold to get a $12 fee reversed. Won. Celebrated with $19 of delivery
bootstrap.body:
I even did the smug little speech to my partner about advocating for yourself and how the small stuff adds up.

The fries arrived cold. Net position: minus seven dollars and all of my credibility.

comments.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
comments.body: the victory tax. every win against a bank must be immediately handed over to a delivery app. it's the natural order of things

comments.author: parallel_parker
comments.body: the cold fries are what elevate this from mistake to tragedy

replies.author: loonie_tunes
replies.body: I paid three separate fees for the privilege. I can name them. they visit me at night

comments.author: second_paycheque
comments.body: been there. paid off a credit card once and "treated myself" to a dinner that went straight back on the card. the cycle is undefeated

bootstrap.author: borrowed_ladder
bootstrap.title: Watched one six-minute video and decided I was qualified to replace a kitchen faucet
bootstrap.body:
The video said "first, shut off the water supply." I did. Confidently. The wrong valve.

The part where I understood my mistake and the part where the water understood my mistake happened at very different speeds. Everything from under the sink is now drying on the lawn, and the cat won't come into the kitchen anymore because she was there. She saw it.

Faucet's in, by the way. Zero leaks. Calling it a win with an asterisk the size of a lake.

comments.author: parallel_parker
comments.body: "the water understood my mistake at a different speed" is the truest sentence ever posted on this site

comments.author: left_on_red
comments.body: the cat witness detail took me out. she is going to hold this over you forever

replies.author: borrowed_ladder
replies.body: she sits at the doorway now and looks at the sink, then at me. nobody has judged me harder, and that includes the water

comments.author: sump_pump_sam
comments.body: 1962 house here. every valve in my basement has a label made of tape because of a day exactly like yours. welcome to the club, the tape's in aisle 9

drip.author: left_on_red
drip.title: Sprinted half a block to catch the streetcar, made it, celebrated. It was going the wrong way
drip.body:
Full arms-up celebration. The driver smiled at me. A woman near the front gave me an actual thumbs up.

Realized at the first stop that it was headed east and I needed west. Rode four more stops anyway, because those people believed in me and I couldn't let them watch me get off that soon.

comments.author: parallel_parker
comments.body: riding four extra stops out of pride is the human condition

comments.author: streetcar_named_delay
comments.body: as this city's unofficial transit biographer: the drivers have seen everything and they remember everyone. you're canon now

replies.author: left_on_red
replies.body: not the legacy I wanted but I'll take it

comments.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
comments.body: you didn't waste a trip, you took a victory lap. reframe it and it's basically free therapy

drip.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
drip.title: Found out my wifi network has been named my old password for three years
drip.body:
Set the router up in a hurry, typed the password into the network name box, hit save, moved on with my life.

Every neighbour in range of my building has been able to read it the entire time. The new password is strong. The shame is stronger.

comments.author: borrowed_ladder
comments.body: this is the digital version of leaving your key in the front door for three years and the door just politely holding itself shut

comments.author: airgapped_alice
comments.body: security person here, cackling respectfully. for what it's worth this is way more common than you'd think, and your fix took five minutes. most people never notice at all

replies.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
replies.body: "most people never notice" is somehow both comforting and a horror story. thank you

comments.author: loonie_tunes
comments.body: meanwhile I've been paying monthly for "premium security" on a router I have never once updated. we are not so different

drip.author: loonie_tunes
drip.title: Cancelled an unused subscription and felt unstoppable. It was my partner's account
drip.body:
She found out mid-workout when her music cut out.

Mine — the one I actually meant to cancel — renewed that same afternoon. Annually. The confirmation email arrived while she was still standing in the kitchen holding her phone, looking at me like a nature documentary.

comments.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
comments.body: your own renewal arriving DURING the confrontation is comedic timing money can't buy. well. it can. it's exactly what it did

comments.author: parallel_parker
comments.body: "looking at me like a nature documentary" — here we observe the loonie, moments after its gravest error

replies.author: loonie_tunes
replies.body: "having wounded its mate, the loonie attempts an offering of takeout. it will not be enough"

comments.author: left_on_red
comments.body: your subscription watching you cancel hers and then renewing itself on schedule. the machines have learned pettiness

late_comments.author: left_on_red
late_comments.body: the patio-guy nod followed by him witnessing your entire downfall is the most this-city thing I've read all week. that patio still talks about you

late_comments.author: borrowed_ladder
late_comments.body: late here, but I once drove 25 minutes to a cheaper gas station. the math has never worked a single time and I will be doing it again this weekend

late_comments.author: ctrl_z_enjoyer
late_comments.body: came back to say I've thought about "the water understood my mistake at a different speed" every day since. it's physics AND poetry
